codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of the typed event system. All 13 event types are correctly defined with proper payloads. EventBus implements the subscription API correctly with panic isolation. Store integration properly emits state change events on successful operations only. 34 total tests (25 in events.rs + 9 in store.rs) provide thorough coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed AGENTS.md conventions: no unsafe code (forbid(unsafe_code) present), thiserror for errors, all public types derive Debug/Clone/Serialize/Deserialize, Send+Sync on public types.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: sd-core/src/events.rs: Event, EventBus implement Send+Sync via Arc/Mutex. All types have proper derives. sd-core/src/lib.rs has #![forbid(unsafe_code)].

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed rust-library-worker skill procedure precisely: wrote tests first (RED), then implementation (GREEN), then lint/fmt. Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows test creation before implementation, TDD pattern visible. Handoff confirms: skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, deviations: [].

sharedStateObservations.observation: Dynamic transition events (ConflictDetected, LifecycleTransition, OscillationDetected, ResolutionAchieved, NeglectDetected, OrientationShift, ConflictResolved) are defined and serializable but are intentionally NOT auto-emitted by pure dynamics computation functions. This is correct architecture - dynamics functions are pure computations without side effects. A higher-level orchestrator (DynamicsEngine, added in subsequent integration-tests feature) should track previous computed state and emit transition events when dynamics change. The handoff correctly documented this as 'whatWasLeftUndone' with clear explanation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: sd-core/src/events.rs lines 75-118: all dynamic transition events defined. sd-core/src/dynamics.rs: pure computation functions return types but emit no events. Handoff notes: 'Dynamic transition events...are fully defined and ready to use, but are not automatically emitted by dynamics computations. This is intentional.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Event emission is correctly placed AFTER transaction commit, ensuring events only fire on successful persistence. This prevents phantom events from failed operations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: sd-core/src/store.rs lines 758-785: update_field emits events after match result block (transaction committed). Lines 585-590 and 654-680: update_status emits events only after COMMIT success.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services needed - events-system is a pure library feature. Store operations already covered by existing test command in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml commands unchanged: test, clippy, fmt_check work for events module without modification.

summary: Events-system feature is fully implemented and passes scrutiny. All 13 event types defined with correct payloads (ULID strings, DateTime timestamps, enum variants). Subscription API works as specified: subscribe() returns handle, dropping handle unsubscribes, multiple subscribers receive identical sequences in causal order. Store integration emits TensionCreated, RealityConfronted, DesireRevised, TensionResolved, TensionReleased, and StructureChanged events on successful operations only. Failed operations emit zero events. Subscriber panic isolation implemented via catch_unwind. 270 tests pass (including 34 event-specific tests). Clippy clean, fmt clean. Dynamic transition events are correctly left for the orchestrator layer (DynamicsEngine) to emit, maintaining pure computations in dynamics module.
